side the town; and in the west i.e. in the afternoon,
he is termed the Toy in, one who marches against the enemy. Mer-
cury, Jupiter and Saturn are always Pauras, while the Moon is
always the Akranda. Ketu, Mars, Rahu and Venus are marching
planets. All the planets known as the Akranda, Tayin and Paura
when hurt, will destroy severally kings coming to the rescue, mar-
ching and slaying in town. When-such planets are victorious, they
bestow victory on their own classes of kings.
[In Sanskrit literature 12 kinds of kings are mentioned. Among
them the Vijigiftt occupies the hub of the wheel. Akranda is next to
the Parsnigraha behind the Vijiglfu. In case Parsnigraha attacks the
central king from behind, the Akranda who is behind the former
could help the chief king by attacking the attacker Tayin and Paura

Planetary Wars XVII 209
are general terms meaning one who attacks or marches, and one
who defends the town respectively. These names do not occur in
the list of 12 kings.
